name: Baked Apple with Maple Yogurt Sauce
recipeInstructions:
+---------------------- MAPLE YOGURT SAUCE -------------------------
A large, firm, tart apple is best. Use Ida Red or Northern Spy. A
Cortland can be used. However, if you use a McIntosh, you must shorten the
cooking time.
1. Core apples and remove top inch of peel. Make a shallow cut through
the skin around the center of each apple to prevent the skin from bursting.
2. Place apples upright in a baking dish or pie plate.
3. In a small bowl, combine brown sugar, raisins, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
4. Spoon the mixture into the centers of each apple.
5. Add water to cover the bottom of the dish.
6. Bake uncovered at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes for less firm apples or up
to 50 minutes for firm varieties, or until apples are tender when pierced with a
toothpick.
7. Combine yogurt and maple syrup in a separate bowl to make the sauce.
8. Pour the sauce over the baked apples.
